Our evidence packet is ready — handing it to Agent B for review.
Round 1: adversarial review
We're checking Agent A's evidence against the rubric. We never see the chart ourselves — only what A quoted, plus outside records like the pharmacy's if we need them.
Acting as the nurse, we called up the pharmacy's dispense records to see what was actually filled — 5 record(s) on file.
Reviewing the packet: criteria 1–3 claim worsening with well-quoted, dated evidence, but

criterion 4 is unassessable and criterion 5 is completely unresolved. Because the worsening claim rests on attribution to disease while adherence is an open question, this is exactly the situation where the dispense feed matters — and it shows ad
alimumab (40 mg SC q2wk) was last dispensed 2026-03-16 with a 28-day supply, so coverage ended 2026-04-13. There are no subsequent adalimumab fills. The entire latest worsening interval (P
ASI 14.2 on 2026-05-10, PASI 16.8 / DLQI 17 on 2026-06-29) postdates that lapse, making non-adherence a concrete, unexcluded confounder overlapping the most

recent deterioration.

data.rubric_quote: Worsening cannot be attributed to disease if a confounder (non-adherence, infection, steroid-withdrawal or seasonal flare) is unaddressed.
data.what_would_satisfy: We'd need documentation that the deterioration started before the biologic coverage lapsed in mid-April, or evidence the severity rise persisted during a period of confirmed adherence — otherwise a concession that the progression can't be separated from the treatment gap.

external_evidence.detail: adalimumab 40 mg SC q2wk, last dispense 2026-03-16, 28-day supply; coverage ended 2026-04-13; no subsequent adalimumab fill before the 2026-05-10 and 2026-06-29 visits documenting the latest worsening
only later fill of any kind is clobetasol ointment on 2026-06-15; no adalimumab refill after 2026-03-16

We concede question 5 — the chart can't answer B's objection, so we're marking it 'not enough documentation'.
Round 2: adversarial review
We're checking Agent A's evidence against the rubric. We never see the chart ourselves — only what A quoted, plus outside records like the pharmacy's if we need them.
We have no objections — the evidence holds up under the rubric.
INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE
We're writing a plain-language text message to the patient about what happens next…
{"message": "Hi, this is a note
from your skin care team. Your psoriasis looks more active, but it seems some doses of your
adalimumab shots may have been missed, so we can't tell the full story yet. Please restart your adalimumab as prescribed,
and reply to let us know about when the flare-up began. We're happy to help if getting the medicine is a problem. — your dermatology care team"}

Hi, this is a note from your skin care team. Your psoriasis looks more active, but it seems some doses of your adalimumab shots may have been missed, so we can't tell the full story yet. Please restart your adalimumab as prescribed, and reply to let us know about when the flare-up began. We're happy to help if getting the medicine is a problem. — your dermatology care team
